CMPSCI-4390 Deep Learning: Project Phase 1¶

Jen Putnam¶

In [1]:
# Upload the image dataset from Google Drive
from google.colab import drive
drive.mount('/content/drive')

Mounted at /content/drive
In [2]:
# Path of image dataset: pass to Image Data Generator
copied_path = "drive/MyDrive/landscapes"

Import Libraries¶

In [3]:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
In [4]:
import PIL
print('Pillow Version:', PIL.__version__)
from PIL import Image

Pillow Version: 7.1.2
In [5]:
from matplotlib.pyplot import imshow
import numpy as np
%matplotlib inline

Example: Image Preprocessing Using Pillow, Matplotlib¶

In [7]:
# load the image
image = Image.open('/content/drive/MyDrive/landscapes/landscapeImg/download (34).jpg')
# summarize some details about the image
print(image.format)
print(image.mode)
print(image.size)

JPEG
RGB
(228, 222)
In [8]:
print(np.asarray(image).shape)
imshow(np.asarray(image))
plt.imshow(np.asarray(image))
plt.colorbar()

(222, 228, 3)
Out[8]:
<matplotlib.colorbar.Colorbar at 0x7fb0b005a290>
In [9]:
# Convert to grayscale
image = Image.open('/content/drive/MyDrive/landscapes/landscapeImg/download (34).jpg')
gs_image = image.convert(mode='L')
imshow(np.asarray(gs_image), cmap='gray')
Out[9]:
<matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7fb0afb7a4d0>
In [10]:
# Create a thumbnail and "preserve aspect ratio"
image = Image.open('/content/drive/MyDrive/landscapes/landscapeImg/download (34).jpg')
image.thumbnail((100,100))
print(image.size)
imshow(np.asarray(image))

(100, 97)
Out[10]:
<matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7fb0ae2eee90>
In [11]:
# Resize and "ignore original aspect ratio"
image = Image.open('/content/drive/MyDrive/landscapes/landscapeImg/download (34).jpg')
img_resized = image.resize((200,200))
print(img_resized.size)
imshow(np.asarray(img_resized))

(200, 200)
Out[11]:
<matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7fb0ae256690>
In [12]:
# Horizontal flip
image = Image.open('/content/drive/MyDrive/landscapes/landscapeImg/download (34).jpg')
imshow(image.transpose(Image.FLIP_LEFT_RIGHT))
Out[12]:
<matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7fb0ae1c7a50>
In [13]:
# Rotate
image = Image.open('/content/drive/MyDrive/landscapes/landscapeImg/download (34).jpg')
imshow(image.rotate(45))
Out[13]:
<matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7fb0b0006f90>

Building ImageDataGenerator¶

In [14]:
from tensorflow.keras.preprocessing.image import ImageDataGenerator
# Rescale all images by 1./255
my_generator = ImageDataGenerator(rescale=1./255)
In [15]:
# Specify how the ImageDataGenerator will read and deliver
my_image_generator = my_generator.flow_from_directory(copied_path, target_size=(150, 150), batch_size=4)

Found 1014 images belonging to 1 classes.
In [17]:
for my_batch in my_image_generator:
    images = my_batch[0]
    for i in range(len(images)):
        # Gives one image
        plt.imshow(images[i])
        plt.colorbar()
        plt.show()
    break

Image Augmentation using ImageDataGenerator¶

In [18]:
my_augmented_generator = ImageDataGenerator(
    width_shift_range=0.2,
    height_shift_range=0.2,
    shear_range=0.1,
    zoom_range=0.2,
    horizontal_flip=True,
    rescale=1./255,
    fill_mode='nearest')
In [19]:
my_augmented_image_generator = my_augmented_generator.flow_from_directory(copied_path, target_size=(150, 150), batch_size=4)

Found 1014 images belonging to 1 classes.
In [21]:
for my_batch in my_augmented_image_generator:
    images = my_batch[0]
    for i in range(len(images)):
        # Gives one image
        plt.imshow(images[i])
        plt.colorbar()
        plt.show()
    break
